prompt: I need to add real-time notifications to our app. Users should see updates when someone comments on their task, assigns them something, etc. Currently we have a REST API backend and React frontend. What's the right architecture for this?

assertions.name: has_constraint_analysis
assertions.description: Analyzes the existing system constraints and notification requirements before recommending

assertions.name: has_architectural_style_recommendation
assertions.description: Recommends a specific real-time approach (WebSocket, SSE, polling) with reasoning

assertions.name: has_alternatives_considered
assertions.description: Compares at least 2 real-time delivery approaches with trade-offs

assertions.name: has_layer_structure
assertions.description: Describes how the notification system integrates with existing REST API and React frontend

assertions.name: has_cross_cutting_concerns
assertions.description: Addresses at least 2 of: auth for persistent connections, error/reconnection handling, scaling WebSocket/SSE connections

assertions.name: has_actionable_next_steps
assertions.description: Provides concrete implementation steps or component breakdown
